mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production growth reflects accelerating real output, which drives corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 50% of months when INDPRO increases, or the rank correlation between INDPRO change and forward returns is negative over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, elevated joblessness constrains wage income and consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 8%, or the mean return during triggered periods exceeds the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: The joint effect of exceptionally low federal funds rates and accelerating industrial production generates equity returns above the sum of their independent effects, as zero-bound monetary policy amplifies the impact of real output recovery on corporate discount rates and earnings.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and INDPRO rank correlation with 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns exclusively during post-recession recoveries when demand revival leads inventory restocking and operating leverage, but not during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in post-2009 recovery data, or the signal produces positive returns with equal frequency in late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly increases in housing starts signal a bottoming construction sector and household formation recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon through housing-sensitive sector earnings.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances following two consecutive monthly increases in HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: Weekly payroll employment changes capture high-frequency labor market momentum that drives consumer spending expectations and near-term equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between month-over-month PAYEMS change and 5-day forward returns is not positive and statistically significant (p < 0.10) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of a declining unemployment rate and positive retail sales growth signals a consumer-driven recovery where job gains translate directly into spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days that neither variable achieves alone.
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction term between UNRATE change and RSAFS change exhibits a rank correlation below 0.03 with 20-day forward returns, or the joint signal underperforms the single-series RSAFS signal.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising consumer prices indicates stagflationary supply constraints, where falling real output meets cost pressures, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average declining while CPIAUCSL 3-month average rising
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered stagflation observations, or the interaction term rank IC is positive.
